## (a) Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}
```

## (b) Lemma specification

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind          // kind never changes
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value        // numeric value never changes
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == (if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
                                                   // width is left-padded to a minimum of PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))             // result is itself wellformed
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD            // result meets the minimum width
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)  // idempotent (already-canonical is a no-op)
{ }
```

### Why this is precise and complete

- **Kind never changes / value never changes** — the two invariants that guarantee canonicalization refers to the same entity.
- **Width result** is pinned down exactly: `max(x.width, PAD)`. This simultaneously encodes:
  - an id already at ≥ 4 digits (`x.width >= PAD`) is returned **unchanged** (`width == x.width`);
  - otherwise it is padded to exactly `PAD` (4) digits — never more, never less.
- **`Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))`** — the result can hold its value (follows since width only grows or stays, and `x` was wellformed).
- **`width >= PAD`** — the canonical form always meets the 4-digit minimum.
- **Idempotence** — canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op, stated directly.

The `width` equality clause alone fully determines the width in every case; the remaining clauses make the entity-preservation, wellformedness, minimum-width, and idempotence guarantees explicit so the contract is captured completely.
